title: Lithocarpus omalokos
taxonomy: Lithocarpus omalokos is classified in the kingdom Plantae, belonging to the phylum Tracheophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Fagales, family Fagaceae, and genus Lithocarpus. It is a species of tree that is native to the Philippines. The family Fagaceae, which it belongs to, is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as beech or oak trees. The genus Lithocarpus, on the other hand, is a group of evergreen trees that are native to tropical Asia. The specific classification of Lithocarpus omalokos is complex and may vary depending on the source.
uses: Lithocarpus omalokos, a species of tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and seeds of the tree are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In folk medicine, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Lithocarpus omalokos.

preparations: Lithocarpus omalokos is a plant species used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations may involve the use of its leaves, bark, or roots. intro: Lithocarpus omalokos, commonly known as the Philippine tanbark oak, is a plant species belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus omalokos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus omalokos is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
